samples.xml_description:
```xml
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. Accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. Supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state name="IDLE" value="2'b00"/>
        <state name="DIVIDE" value="2'b01"/>
        <state name="DONE" value="2'b10"/>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="SR" width="9" init_value="0">Shift Register.</reg>
        <reg id="NEG_DIVISOR" width="9" init_value="0">Negative Divisor.</reg>
        <reg id="CNT" width="4" init_value="0">Counter.</reg>
        <reg id="START_CNT" width="1" init_value="0">Start Counter.</reg>
        <reg id="RESULT" width="16" init_value="0">Final Result.</reg>
        <reg id="RES_VALID" width="1" init_value="0">Result Valid Signal.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Initialize registers on reset
        always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
            if (rst) begin
                SR <= 9'd0;
                NEG_DIVISOR <= 9'd0;
                CNT <= 4'd0;
                START_CNT <= 1'b0;
                RESULT <= 16'd0;
                RES_VALID <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if (opn_valid && !RES_VALID) begin
                    // Save inputs
                    SR[7:0] <= dividend;
                    SR[8] <= 1'b0; // Shift left by one bit
                    NEG_DIVISOR[7:0] <= (sign == 1'b1) ? ~divisor + 1 : divisor;
                    NEG_DIVISOR[8] <= 1'b0; // Sign extension
                    CNT <= 4'd1;
                    START_CNT <= 1'b1;
                end else if (START_CNT) begin
                    if (CNT[3]) begin
                        // Division complete
                        START_CNT <= 1'b0;
                        RESULT[15:8] <= SR[7:0]; // Remainder
                        RESULT[7:0] <= SR[15:8]; // Quotient
                    end else begin
                        // Perform division step
                        CNT <= CNT + 1;
                        {SR[8], SR[7:0]} <= SR[8] ? SR[7:0] - NEG_DIVISOR[7:0] : SR[7:0];
                        SR[8] <= SR[8] ^ SR[7]; // Carry-out
                    end
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Manage result validity
        always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
            if (rst) begin
                RES_VALID <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if (CNT[3]) begin
                    RES_VALID <= 1'b1;
                end else if (RES_VALID && res_ready) begin
                    RES_VALID <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
```
